The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]]

SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}} [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥}}, which is now the {{ill|Sumitomo Group|ja|住友グループ}}. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]]

The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]]

{{infobox Building | Name = Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building
(Former: Sumitomo Building) | Former Name = Sumitomo Bank Main Building {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Image = [[File:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] | Use = Bank, Auditorium {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Former Use = Bank, Office | Architect = {{ill|Sumitomo Partnership|ja|住友合資会社}} Construction Department ({{ill|Hidaka Man|ja|日高胖}}, {{ill|Hasebe Eikichi|ja|長谷部鋭吉}}, {{ill|Takekoshi Kenzo|ja|竹腰健造}}) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Structural Engineer = | Builder = {{ill|Obayashi Corporation|ja|大林組}} {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Owner = Sumitomo Partnership | Structure Type = {{ill|Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|ja|鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造}} | Site Area = | Building Area = Approx. 6,668 [[square meters|m{{sup|2}}]] | Total Floor Area = Approx. 35,983 m{{sup|2}} | Floors = 1 basement floor, 6 above-ground floors {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Height = Approx. 24.2 meters | Construction Start = | Completion = [[1926]] (Phase 1), [[1930]] (Phase 2) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Renovation = [[1964]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}}, [[2015]]"North Hamamatsu Sumitomo Building to be preserved; SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}} [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥}}, which is now the {{ill|Sumitomo Group|ja|住友グループ}}. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]]

{{infobox Building | Name = Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building
(Former: Sumitomo Building) | Former Name = Sumitomo Bank Main Building {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Image = [[File:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] | Use = Bank, Auditorium {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Former Use = Bank, Office | Architect = {{ill|Sumitomo Partnership|ja|住友合資会社}} Construction Department ({{ill|Hidaka Man|ja|日高胖}}, {{ill|Hasebe Eikichi|ja|長谷部鋭吉}}, {{ill|Takekoshi Kenzo|ja|竹腰健造}}) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Structural Engineer = | Builder = {{ill|Obayashi Corporation|ja|大林組}} {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Owner = Sumitomo Partnership | Structure Type = {{ill|Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|ja|鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造}} | Site Area = | Building Area = Approx. 6,668 [[square meters|m{{sup|2}}]] | Total Floor Area = Approx. 35,983 m{{sup|2}} | Floors = 1 basement floor, 6 above-ground floors {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Height = Approx. 24.2 meters | Construction Start = | Completion = [[1926]] (Phase 1), [[1930]] (Phase 2) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Renovation = [[1964]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}}, [[2015]]"North Hamamatsu Sumitomo Building to be preserved; SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}} [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)}}. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥}}, which is now the {{ill|Sumitomo Group|ja|住友グループ}}. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]]

Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長|12=draft}} (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山|12=draft}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|12=draft}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|12=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|12=draft}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|12=draft}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅|12=draft}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅|12=draft}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長|12=draft}} (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山|12=draft}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|12=draft}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|12=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|12=draft}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|12=draft}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅|12=draft}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅|12=draft}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[1926]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]]

{{建築物 |名称 = 三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル
（旧：住友ビルディング） |旧名称 = 住友銀行本店ビル{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}} |画像 = [[画像:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] |用途 = 銀行、講堂{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}} |旧用途 = 銀行、事務所 |設計者 = [[住友合資会社]]工作部（[[日高胖]]・[[長谷部鋭吉]]・[[竹腰健造]]）{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}} |構造設計者 = |施工 = [[大林組]]{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}} |建築主 = 住友合資会社 |事業主体 = |管理運営 = |構造形式 = [[鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造]] |敷地面積 = |建築面積 = |建築面積ref = 約6,668 [[平方メートル|m{{sup|2}}]] |建築面積備考 = |延床面積 = |延床面積ref = 約35,983 m{{sup|2}} |延床面積備考 = |階数 = 地下1階、地上6階{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}} |高さ = 約24.2メートル |着工 = |竣工 = [[1926年]]4月(第1期)、[[1930年]]7月(第2期){{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}} |開館開所 = |改築 = [[1964年]]{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}}、[[2015年]]「北浜 住友ビル保存へ 三井住友銀大阪本店モダン建築の価値重視」『読売新聞』大阪夕刊 2013年5月25日 |所在地郵便番号 = 541-0041 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[北浜]]四丁目6番5号 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 41 | 緯度秒 = 31 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 53 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |位置図種類 = Osaka city |文化財指定 = |指定日 = |備考 = }} '''三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル'''（みついすみともぎんこうおおさかほんてんビル）は、[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[北浜]]に所在する[[三井住友銀行]]の建物。旧名称は、'''住友ビルディング'''（すみともビルディング）、'''[[住友銀行]]本店ビル'''。 == 概要 == 旧[[住友財閥]]（現[[住友グループ]]）の拠点として建てられた建築物。当ビル周辺は住友グループ各社の本社ビルが立ち並ぶことから通称「'''[[住友村]]'''」と呼ばれ、戦前は旧住友本社も置かれたことから住友村の中核的建造物である{{Cite web|和書|title=三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル {{!}} 住友の歴史 {{!}} 住友グループ広報委員会 |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |website=www.sumitomo.gr.jp |access-date=2022-08-12 |language=ja}}。 [[住友家]]の事業は、[[江戸時代]]の前、[[慶長]]年間の[[京都]]に端を発し、[[1623年]]([[元和 (日本)|元和]]9年）に大阪に移り、長堀（鰻谷）で「南蛮吹き」銅構造を家業として栄えてきた{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=39}}。幕末の政変、新政府の出現によって、大阪は一時苦境に立たされ、住友家も他の[[豪商]]と同じように苦しい局面があったが、[[別子銅山]]の近代化を図りながら体制を建て直し、大阪の名門企業として成長を続けた{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=39}}。ただ住友家は家法に「浮利は追わず」の経営理念があり、[[銀行業]]については大きく立ち遅れていた{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=39}}。 [[画像:千里山学舎大学本館 (1).jpg|200px|thumb|left|旧住友合資本社社屋
（1927年に[[関西大学]]に譲渡・移築され、同大学本館として1954年まで使用された）[https://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/nenshi/history/detail.php?cd=19&nm=1 大学本館｜関西大学 年史編纂室]]] [[1882年]]（[[明治]]25年）のちに総領事となる[[伊庭貞剛]]は、銀行業を興すことを定めるため[[尾道]]で重役会議を開いて議決、[[1885年]]（明治28年）住友銀行を設立する{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=39}}。[[1920年]]（[[大正]]9年）、[[住友総本店]]では[[東区 (大阪市)|東区]][[北浜]]、現在の[[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]北浜の本店所有の空き地に、本店ビルを新築する計画を具体化した{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=129}}。当時、北浜の敷地には南側に[[1908年]]（明治41年）11月につくられた建坪640坪2階建ての洋館があり、2階を住友本店、1階を銀行本店が使用していた。しかし、[[第1次世界大戦]]を経て銀行の業容が拡大すると、この建物では手狭になったため、[[1918年]]（大正7年）12月に本部と営業部を分け{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=129}}、営業部を別の建物に移した。ちょうどその頃、大阪市内に[[官公庁]]や[[百貨店]]など近代的ビルが建ち始めたこともあり、住友本店では連系会社の一部をも収容できる新しいビルの建築を決断した{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。 ビル建設あたって十五代当主の[[住友友純|住友吉左衞門友純]]は、住友本店に設計部門をつくり（住友総本店営繕課、[[住友合資会社]]工作部を経て現在の[[日建設計]]）{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=42}}、[[野口孫市]]（本建物着工前に死去）・[[日高胖]]・[[長谷部鋭吉]]・[[竹腰健造]]が設計を担い[http://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/magazine/feature03/ 住友の都市美学「住友営繕」とその背景](住友グループ広報委員会)、主体工事は[[大林組]]が請け負うことになった{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。 工事は二期に分け、まず南側に本店建物を残し北半分に上層部を貸事務所とする7階建てのビルを建設して、そののち木造の本店を取り壊して南半分に同規模の建物を建築する計画を立て、第一期工事は予定通り[[1922年]]（大正11年）12月に起工した{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。ところが直後に[[関東大震災]]が起こった。友純は震災後の焼け跡を回ってその惨状をまのあたりにし{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=42}}、建築計画の見直しを指示した。その結果、最終的に7階は廃して6階部分の面積は半減し、防災、避難体制を考慮して外部への賃貸をとりやめ{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}、第一期工事は[[1926年]]（大正15年）4月に竣工、第二期工事は[[1930年]]（昭和5年）7月に竣工し、住友ビルディングは完成した{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。 ビルは1、2階を住友銀行、[[住友信託]]が使用し、3、4階に住友合資会社、4階と5階の一部に連系各社が入り、6階には食堂が設けられた{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。堅実もむねとする住友の家訓にふさわしく、あくまで建築は耐火耐震構造を第一義とした{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=131}}。 === 終戦後 === 終戦直後、[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部]] (GHQ) は、住友ビルディングに第六軍第一軍団の司令部を置くことを決定し、住友本社に対して速やかに引き渡すよう通知してきた{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=222}}。当初、GHQは住友ビル全館を[[接収]]すると通告してきたが、GHQと交渉した住友土地工務専務だった竹腰が銀行や信託の使用している1、2階まで接収されると、一般大衆と深くかかわっている金融業務に重大な支障を及ぼし、大阪経済を大混乱に陥れかねないとして断固拒否したため、5階と食堂のある6階だけの接収で決着したという{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=222}}。 昭和30年代に入ると、住友ビルディングも入居各社の発展に連れて過密状態になり{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=262}}、各社協議の結果、ビル東側に隣接する[[住友商事]]および住友家所有の土地約9,000㎡の敷地に地下4階地上12階の新ビルを建設することが決定し、[[1959年]]（昭和34年）9月に着工、[[1962年]]（昭和37年）7月に竣工した。この新ビルは「新住友ビルディング」（現「[[住友ビルディング]]」）と呼ばれることになった{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}}。同ビルの完成を受け、住友ビルディングは10月から住友銀行の単独所有となり、それを機に設計を日建設計工務（現：[[日建設計]]）、施工を大林組、電気工事を[[住友電気工業]]の担当で、改造工事に入り、屋上の中庭のあった所に400人の収容の[[講堂]]を増築したほか本店営業部の改装を行い、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）末までに付属設備の更新工事も完成させた{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}}。 なお、ビルは[[1982年]]（昭和57年）7月から住友銀行本店ビルと呼ぶようになり{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}}、[[2001年]]（平成13年）4月の[[さくら銀行]]との合併を経て、三井住友銀行大阪本店ビルと称するようになった。 === 2度目の大規模改修 === [[三井住友銀行]]は、2000年代後半から[[耐震性]]の向上やスペースの有効利用を図るため、大阪本店ビルの約50年ぶりとなる大規模改修の検討を始め、建て替える案や、東京・[[銀座]]の[[歌舞伎座]]のように後方に高層ビルを建てる案なども議論されたが、歴史的価値のある建物をそのまま残すべきだという意見が多く、また[[阪神・淡路大震災]]でも損傷せず、現在の建築基準に照らしても十分の強度があることが分かり、建て替えはしないとの結論に至った。これに則り、[[2015年]]（平成27年）5月末までの予定で改修工事に取り掛かり、内側の柱の間に板を渡し耐震性をさらに高めたほか、川の氾濫に備えて、高さ1.2mの[[防潮板]]を造り、1階には建設当初からある天井の[[ステンドグラス]]を生かして待合室も新設された{{Cite news |title=光の天井でお出迎え　三井住友銀行、大阪本店の改修終える |author= |publisher=日本経済新聞 |date=2015-05-20 |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/ |accessdate=2021-01-12}}。3635枚の米国製ガラスからなる天井のステンドグラスは1989年（平成元年）に雨漏りのシートで覆われたことで見えにくくなっていた{{Cite web |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20150519-W3ISRCXNONNUNOKVTWDPYSDEYY/ |title=ガラス３６００枚を張り巡らせた豪華ステンドグラスもお披露目　三井住友銀行大阪本店ビルの耐震工事が完成 |publisher =産経新聞 |date=2015-05-19 |accessdate=2024-07-06}}。 == 意匠 == 近世復興様式の重厚な建物は{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}、黄土色の[[竜山石]]で窓は小さく、重厚感のある[[ファサード]]で、[[土佐堀通]]、[[魚の棚筋]]、旧[[西横堀川]]側の玄関部分に[[イオニア式]][[オーダー (建築)|オーダー]]と、英文での社名看板、南の今橋側のファサードはアーチ窓とメダリオンといった[[意匠]]となっている。銀行営業室の内装には磨き上げた[[トラバーチン]]による[[コリント式]]オーダーが立ち並び、黒[[大理石]]のカウンターや幾何学的な天井の装飾などシンプルな外観から一転した華麗な意匠となっている{{Cite web |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |title=三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル |publisher =住友グループ広報委員会 |accessdate=2024-07-06}}。 [[1999年]]（平成11年）には日本の近代建築20選（[[DOCOMOMO JAPAN選定 日本におけるモダン・ムーブメントの建築]]）に選ばれている。また、[[生きた建築ミュージアム]]・大阪セレクションに選定された。 == 周辺 == * [[住友村]] - [[住友グループ]]各社の本社や大阪の拠点などがある。 ** [[住友ビルディング]] - 東側に隣接するビル。旧「新住友ビルディング」。 * [[大阪倶楽部]] * [[日本銀行大阪支店]] * [[フェスティバルホール]] - [[中之島フェスティバルタワー]] * [[三井住友銀行大阪中央支店]] - [[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[高麗橋]]の旧[[三井銀行]]大阪営業部で、三井銀行の大阪の拠点だった。 == 交通アクセス == * [[Osaka Metro四つ橋線]][[肥後橋駅]] 徒歩3分 * [[Osaka Metro御堂筋線]]・[[京阪本線]][[淀屋橋駅]] 徒歩4分 * [[京阪中之島線]][[大江橋駅]] 徒歩7分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=住友銀行行史編纂委員会編|title=住友銀行百年史|publisher=住友銀行|date=1998-08|isbn=|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|title=建築と社会|publisher=日本建築協会|date=2010-09|isbn=|ref=harv}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ 三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル] - 住友グループ広報委員会 {{大阪都市景観建築賞第35～39回}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みついすみともきんこうおおさかほんてんひる}} [[Category:住友グループ]] [[Category:占領軍に接収された日本の建築物]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:三井住友フィナンシャルグループ|建おおさかほんてんひる]] [[Category:日本におけるモダン・ムーブメントの建築選]] [[Category:1926年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:1930年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:日本の新古典主義建築]] [[Category:船場]] [[Category:北浜]] [[Category:日本の銀行建築]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] to [[Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{建築物 |名称 = 三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル
（旧：住友ビルディング） |旧名称 = 住友銀行本店ビル{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}} |画像 = [[画像:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] |用途 = 銀行、講堂{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}} |旧用途 = 銀行、事務所 |設計者 = [[住友合資会社]]工作部（[[日高胖]]・[[長谷部鋭吉]]・[[竹腰健造]]）{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}} |構造設計者 = |施工 = [[大林組]]{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}} |建築主 = 住友合資会社 |事業主体 = |管理運営 = |構造形式 = [[鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造]] |敷地面積 = |建築面積 = |建築面積ref = 約6,668 [[平方メートル|m{{sup|2}}]] |建築面積備考 = |延床面積 = |延床面積ref = 約35,983 m{{sup|2}} |延床面積備考 = |階数 = 地下1階、地上6階{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}} |高さ = 約24.2メートル |着工 = |竣工 = [[1926年]]4月(第1期)、[[1930年]]7月(第2期){{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}} |開館開所 = |改築 = [[1964年]]{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}}、[[2015年]]「北浜 住友ビル保存へ 三井住友銀大阪本店モダン建築の価値重視」『読売新聞』大阪夕刊 2013年5月25日 |所在地郵便番号 = 541-0041 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[北浜]]四丁目6番5号 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 41 | 緯度秒 = 31 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 53 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |位置図種類 = Osaka city |文化財指定 = |指定日 = |備考 = }} '''三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル'''（みついすみともぎんこうおおさかほんてんビル）は、[[大阪市]][[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[北浜]]に所在する[[三井住友銀行]]の建物。旧名称は、'''住友ビルディング'''（すみともビルディング）、'''[[住友銀行]]本店ビル'''。 == 概要 == 旧[[住友財閥]]（現[[住友グループ]]）の拠点として建てられた建築物。当ビル周辺は住友グループ各社の本社ビルが立ち並ぶことから通称「'''[[住友村]]'''」と呼ばれ、戦前は旧住友本社も置かれたことから住友村の中核的建造物である{{Cite web|和書|title=三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル {{!}} 住友の歴史 {{!}} 住友グループ広報委員会 |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |website=www.sumitomo.gr.jp |access-date=2022-08-12 |language=ja}}。 [[住友家]]の事業は、[[江戸時代]]の前、[[慶長]]年間の[[京都]]に端を発し、[[1623年]]([[元和 (日本)|元和]]9年）に大阪に移り、長堀（鰻谷）で「南蛮吹き」銅構造を家業として栄えてきた{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=39}}。幕末の政変、新政府の出現によって、大阪は一時苦境に立たされ、住友家も他の[[豪商]]と同じように苦しい局面があったが、[[別子銅山]]の近代化を図りながら体制を建て直し、大阪の名門企業として成長を続けた{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=39}}。ただ住友家は家法に「浮利は追わず」の経営理念があり、[[銀行業]]については大きく立ち遅れていた{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=39}}。 [[画像:千里山学舎大学本館 (1).jpg|200px|thumb|left|旧住友合資本社社屋
（1927年に[[関西大学]]に譲渡・移築され、同大学本館として1954年まで使用された）[https://www.kansai-u.ac.jp/nenshi/history/detail.php?cd=19&nm=1 大学本館｜関西大学 年史編纂室]]] [[1882年]]（[[明治]]25年）のちに総領事となる[[伊庭貞剛]]は、銀行業を興すことを定めるため[[尾道]]で重役会議を開いて議決、[[1885年]]（明治28年）住友銀行を設立する{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=39}}。[[1920年]]（[[大正]]9年）、[[住友総本店]]では[[東区 (大阪市)|東区]][[北浜]]、現在の[[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]北浜の本店所有の空き地に、本店ビルを新築する計画を具体化した{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=129}}。当時、北浜の敷地には南側に[[1908年]]（明治41年）11月につくられた建坪640坪2階建ての洋館があり、2階を住友本店、1階を銀行本店が使用していた。しかし、[[第1次世界大戦]]を経て銀行の業容が拡大すると、この建物では手狭になったため、[[1918年]]（大正7年）12月に本部と営業部を分け{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=129}}、営業部を別の建物に移した。ちょうどその頃、大阪市内に[[官公庁]]や[[百貨店]]など近代的ビルが建ち始めたこともあり、住友本店では連系会社の一部をも収容できる新しいビルの建築を決断した{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。 ビル建設あたって十五代当主の[[住友友純|住友吉左衞門友純]]は、住友本店に設計部門をつくり（住友総本店営繕課、[[住友合資会社]]工作部を経て現在の[[日建設計]]）{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=42}}、[[野口孫市]]（本建物着工前に死去）・[[日高胖]]・[[長谷部鋭吉]]・[[竹腰健造]]が設計を担い[http://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/magazine/feature03/ 住友の都市美学「住友営繕」とその背景](住友グループ広報委員会)、主体工事は[[大林組]]が請け負うことになった{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。 工事は二期に分け、まず南側に本店建物を残し北半分に上層部を貸事務所とする7階建てのビルを建設して、そののち木造の本店を取り壊して南半分に同規模の建物を建築する計画を立て、第一期工事は予定通り[[1922年]]（大正11年）12月に起工した{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。ところが直後に[[関東大震災]]が起こった。友純は震災後の焼け跡を回ってその惨状をまのあたりにし{{Sfn|建築と社会|2010|p=42}}、建築計画の見直しを指示した。その結果、最終的に7階は廃して6階部分の面積は半減し、防災、避難体制を考慮して外部への賃貸をとりやめ{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}、第一期工事は[[1926年]]（大正15年）4月に竣工、第二期工事は[[1930年]]（昭和5年）7月に竣工し、住友ビルディングは完成した{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。 ビルは1、2階を住友銀行、[[住友信託]]が使用し、3、4階に住友合資会社、4階と5階の一部に連系各社が入り、6階には食堂が設けられた{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}。堅実もむねとする住友の家訓にふさわしく、あくまで建築は耐火耐震構造を第一義とした{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=131}}。 === 終戦後 === 終戦直後、[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部]] (GHQ) は、住友ビルディングに第六軍第一軍団の司令部を置くことを決定し、住友本社に対して速やかに引き渡すよう通知してきた{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=222}}。当初、GHQは住友ビル全館を[[接収]]すると通告してきたが、GHQと交渉した住友土地工務専務だった竹腰が銀行や信託の使用している1、2階まで接収されると、一般大衆と深くかかわっている金融業務に重大な支障を及ぼし、大阪経済を大混乱に陥れかねないとして断固拒否したため、5階と食堂のある6階だけの接収で決着したという{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=222}}。 昭和30年代に入ると、住友ビルディングも入居各社の発展に連れて過密状態になり{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=262}}、各社協議の結果、ビル東側に隣接する[[住友商事]]および住友家所有の土地約9,000㎡の敷地に地下4階地上12階の新ビルを建設することが決定し、[[1959年]]（昭和34年）9月に着工、[[1962年]]（昭和37年）7月に竣工した。この新ビルは「新住友ビルディング」（現「[[住友ビルディング]]」）と呼ばれることになった{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}}。同ビルの完成を受け、住友ビルディングは10月から住友銀行の単独所有となり、それを機に設計を日建設計工務（現：[[日建設計]]）、施工を大林組、電気工事を[[住友電気工業]]の担当で、改造工事に入り、屋上の中庭のあった所に400人の収容の[[講堂]]を増築したほか本店営業部の改装を行い、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）末までに付属設備の更新工事も完成させた{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}}。 なお、ビルは[[1982年]]（昭和57年）7月から住友銀行本店ビルと呼ぶようになり{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=263}}、[[2001年]]（平成13年）4月の[[さくら銀行]]との合併を経て、三井住友銀行大阪本店ビルと称するようになった。 === 2度目の大規模改修 === [[三井住友銀行]]は、2000年代後半から[[耐震性]]の向上やスペースの有効利用を図るため、大阪本店ビルの約50年ぶりとなる大規模改修の検討を始め、建て替える案や、東京・[[銀座]]の[[歌舞伎座]]のように後方に高層ビルを建てる案なども議論されたが、歴史的価値のある建物をそのまま残すべきだという意見が多く、また[[阪神・淡路大震災]]でも損傷せず、現在の建築基準に照らしても十分の強度があることが分かり、建て替えはしないとの結論に至った。これに則り、[[2015年]]（平成27年）5月末までの予定で改修工事に取り掛かり、内側の柱の間に板を渡し耐震性をさらに高めたほか、川の氾濫に備えて、高さ1.2mの[[防潮板]]を造り、1階には建設当初からある天井の[[ステンドグラス]]を生かして待合室も新設された{{Cite news |title=光の天井でお出迎え　三井住友銀行、大阪本店の改修終える |author= |publisher=日本経済新聞 |date=2015-05-20 |url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/ |accessdate=2021-01-12}}。3635枚の米国製ガラスからなる天井のステンドグラスは1989年（平成元年）に雨漏りのシートで覆われたことで見えにくくなっていた{{Cite web |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20150519-W3ISRCXNONNUNOKVTWDPYSDEYY/ |title=ガラス３６００枚を張り巡らせた豪華ステンドグラスもお披露目　三井住友銀行大阪本店ビルの耐震工事が完成 |publisher =産経新聞 |date=2015-05-19 |accessdate=2024-07-06}}。 == 意匠 == 近世復興様式の重厚な建物は{{Sfn|住友銀行百年史|1998|p=130}}、黄土色の[[竜山石]]で窓は小さく、重厚感のある[[ファサード]]で、[[土佐堀通]]、[[魚の棚筋]]、旧[[西横堀川]]側の玄関部分に[[イオニア式]][[オーダー (建築)|オーダー]]と、英文での社名看板、南の今橋側のファサードはアーチ窓とメダリオンといった[[意匠]]となっている。銀行営業室の内装には磨き上げた[[トラバーチン]]による[[コリント式]]オーダーが立ち並び、黒[[大理石]]のカウンターや幾何学的な天井の装飾などシンプルな外観から一転した華麗な意匠となっている{{Cite web |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |title=三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル |publisher =住友グループ広報委員会 |accessdate=2024-07-06}}。 [[1999年]]（平成11年）には日本の近代建築20選（[[DOCOMOMO JAPAN選定 日本におけるモダン・ムーブメントの建築]]）に選ばれている。また、[[生きた建築ミュージアム]]・大阪セレクションに選定された。 == 周辺 == * [[住友村]] - [[住友グループ]]各社の本社や大阪の拠点などがある。 ** [[住友ビルディング]] - 東側に隣接するビル。旧「新住友ビルディング」。 * [[大阪倶楽部]] * [[日本銀行大阪支店]] * [[フェスティバルホール]] - [[中之島フェスティバルタワー]] * [[三井住友銀行大阪中央支店]] - [[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]][[高麗橋]]の旧[[三井銀行]]大阪営業部で、三井銀行の大阪の拠点だった。 == 交通アクセス == * [[Osaka Metro四つ橋線]][[肥後橋駅]] 徒歩3分 * [[Osaka Metro御堂筋線]]・[[京阪本線]][[淀屋橋駅]] 徒歩4分 * [[京阪中之島線]][[大江橋駅]] 徒歩7分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=住友銀行行史編纂委員会編|title=住友銀行百年史|publisher=住友銀行|date=1998-08|isbn=|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|title=建築と社会|publisher=日本建築協会|date=2010-09|isbn=|ref=harv}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ 三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル] - 住友グループ広報委員会 {{大阪都市景観建築賞第35～39回}} [[Category:1926年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:1930年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:日本の新古典主義建築]] [[Category:船場]] [[Category:北浜]] [[Category:日本の銀行建築]]

{{infobox building | Name = Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building
(Former: Sumitomo Building) | Former Name = Sumitomo Bank Main Building {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | image = [[File:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] | Use = Bank, Auditorium {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Former Use = Bank, Office | Architect = {{ill|Sumitomo Partnership|ja|住友合資会社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Construction Department ({{ill|Hidaka Man|ja|日高胖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hasebe Eikichi|ja|長谷部鋭吉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takekoshi Kenzo|ja|竹腰健造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Structural Engineer = | Builder = {{ill|Obayashi Corporation|ja|大林組|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Owner = Sumitomo Partnership | Structure Type = {{ill|Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|ja|鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Site Area = | Building Area = Approx. 6,668 [[square meters|m{{sup|2}}]] | Total Floor Area = Approx. 35,983 m{{sup|2}} | Floors = 1 basement floor, 6 above-ground floors {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Height = Approx. 24.2 meters | Construction Start = | Completion tier 3]] (Phase 1), [[1930]] (Phase 2) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Renovation = [[1964]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}}, [[2015]]"North Hamamatsu Sumitomo Building to be preserved; SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is now the {{ill|Sumitomo Group|ja|住友グループ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is now the {{ill|Sumitomo Group|ja|住友グループ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō-ku, Osaka|qq=}} [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|qq=}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|ja|中央区 (大阪市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō-ku, Osaka|qq=}}. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|qq=}}, which is now the {{ill|Sumitomo Group|ja|住友グループ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Group|qq=}}. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō era|qq=}} (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the {{ill|Besshi Copper Mine|ja|別子銅山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Besshi Copper Mine|qq=}} and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the {{ill|Allied occupation of Japan|ja|連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Allied occupation of Japan|qq=}} (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * {{ill|Higobashi Station|ja|肥後橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higobashi Station|qq=}} (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * {{ill|Yodoyabashi Station|ja|淀屋橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yodoyabashi Station|qq=}} (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[1926]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]]

SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|qq=}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|qq=}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[1926]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]]

The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[1926]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:1926年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:1930年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]]

The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623.
