{{infobox building | Name = Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building
(Former: Sumitomo Building) | Former Name = Sumitomo Bank Main Building {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | image = [[File:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] | Use = Bank, Auditorium {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Former Use = Bank, Office | Architect = {{ill|Sumitomo Partnership|ja|住友合資会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Partnership|qq=}} Construction Department ({{ill|Hidaka Man|ja|日高胖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Man|qq=}}, {{ill|Hasebe Eikichi|ja|長谷部鋭吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe Eikichi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takekoshi Kenzo|ja|竹腰健造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekoshi Kenzo|qq=}}) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Structural Engineer = | Builder = [[:en:Obayashi Corporation|Obayashi Corporation]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Owner = Sumitomo Partnership | Structure Type = {{ill|Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|ja|鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|qq=}} | Site Area = | Building Area = Approx. 6,668 [[square meters|m{{sup|2}}]] | Total Floor Area = Approx. 35,983 m{{sup|2}} | Floors = 1 basement floor, 6 above-ground floors {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Height = Approx. 24.2 meters | Construction Start = | Completion tier 3]] (Phase 1), [[1930]] (Phase 2) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Renovation = [[1964]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}}, [[2015]]"North Hamamatsu Sumitomo Building to be preserved; SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|qq=}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|qq=}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[1926]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:1926年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:1930年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:北浜]] [[Category:占領軍に接収された日本の建築物]] [[Category:大阪市中央区の建築物]]

Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[1926]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1926]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:北浜]] [[Category:占領軍に接収された日本の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:日本におけるモダン・ムーブメントの建築選]] [[Category:日本の銀行建築]] [[Category:船場]]

SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|qq=}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|qq=}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - {{infobox building | Name = Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building
(Former: Sumitomo Building) | Former Name = Sumitomo Bank Main Building {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | image = [[File:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] | Use = Bank, Auditorium {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Former Use = Bank, Office | Architect = {{ill|Sumitomo Partnership|ja|住友合資会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Partnership|qq=}} Construction Department ({{ill|Hidaka Man|ja|日高胖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Man|qq=}}, {{ill|Hasebe Eikichi|ja|長谷部鋭吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe Eikichi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takekoshi Kenzo|ja|竹腰健造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekoshi Kenzo|qq=}}) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Structural Engineer = | Builder = [[:en:Obayashi Corporation|Obayashi Corporation]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Owner = Sumitomo Partnership | Structure Type = {{ill|Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|ja|鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|qq=}} | Site Area = | Building Area = Approx. 6,668 [[square meters|m{{sup|2}}]] | Total Floor Area = Approx. 35,983 m{{sup|2}} | Floors = 1 basement floor, 6 above-ground floors {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Height = Approx. 24.2 meters | Construction Start = | Completion tier 3]] (Phase 1), [[1930]] (Phase 2) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Renovation = [[1964]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}}, [[2015]]"North Hamamatsu Sumitomo Building to be preserved; SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|qq=}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|qq=}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[1926]] [[Category:Buildings occupied by Allied forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1926]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Kitahama, Osaka]] [[Category:占領軍に接収された日本の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:日本におけるモダン・ムーブメントの建築選]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:船場]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]]

SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|qq=}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|qq=}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[1926]] [[Category:Modern Movement architecture in Japan]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1926]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Kitahama, Osaka]] [[Category:占領軍に接収された日本の建築物]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:日本におけるモダン・ムーブメントの建築選]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:船場]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]]

The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[:en:1926|1926]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1926]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Kitahama, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings Occupied by Allied Forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:日本におけるモダン・ムーブメントの建築選]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:船場]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]]

The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category: = [[:en:1926|1926]] [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[sn:Sumitomo Bank Osaka Head Office Building]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1926]] [[Category:Built in Japan in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Kitahama, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings Occupied by Allied Forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement Architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Semba]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]]

{{infobox building | Name = Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building
(Former: Sumitomo Building) | Former Name = Sumitomo Bank Main Building {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | image = [[File:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] | Use = Bank, Auditorium {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Former Use = Bank, Office | Architect = {{ill|Sumitomo Partnership|ja|住友合資会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Partnership|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住友財閥}} Construction Department ({{ill|Hidaka Man|ja|日高胖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Man|qq=}}, {{ill|Hasebe Eikichi|ja|長谷部鋭吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe Eikichi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takekoshi Kenzo|ja|竹腰健造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekoshi Kenzo|qq=}}) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Structural Engineer = | Builder = [[:en:Obayashi Corporation|Obayashi Corporation]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Owner = Sumitomo Partnership | Structure Type = {{ill|Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|ja|鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|qq=}} | Site Area = | Building Area = Approx. 6,668 [[square meters|m{{sup|2}}]] | Total Floor Area = Approx. 35,983 m{{sup|2}} | Floors = 1 basement floor, 6 above-ground floors {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Height = Approx. 24.2 meters | Construction Start = | Completion tier 3]] (Phase 1), [[1930]] (Phase 2) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Renovation = [[1964]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}}, [[2015]]"North Hamamatsu Sumitomo Building to be preserved; SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|qq=}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|qq=}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=|1=Sumitomo Building|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96971400}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=|1=Osaka Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11440050}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=|1=Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108912361}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - based on wikidata|WD=Q10885869}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=|1=Sumitomo-mura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381763}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=|1=Sumitomo Building|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96971400}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=|1=Osaka Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11440050}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=|1=Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108912361}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|qq=|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|qq=|1=Sumitomo zaibatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885869}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|qq=|1=Sumitomo-mura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381763}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|qq=|1=Sumitomo Building|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96971400}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|qq=|1=Osaka Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11440050}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|qq=|1=Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108912361}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1926]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Kitahama, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings Occupied by Allied Forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement Architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Semba]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{wikidata link|Q11381732}}

SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|1=Sumitomo zaibatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885869}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|1=Sumitomo-mura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381763}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|1=Sumitomo Building|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96971400}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|1=Osaka Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11440050}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|1=Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108912361}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1926]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Kitahama, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings Occupied by Allied Forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement Architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Semba]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{wikidata link|Q11381732}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Sumitomo Building (Osaka)]][[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]][[zh:住友大廈]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381732]] Wikidata name: Sumitomo Building [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox building | Name = Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building
(Former: Sumitomo Building) | Former Name = Sumitomo Bank Main Building {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | image = [[File:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] | Use = Bank, Auditorium {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Former Use = Bank, Office | Architect = {{ill|Sumitomo Partnership|ja|住友合資会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Partnership|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住友財閥|1=Sumitomo zaibatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885869}} Construction Department ({{ill|Hidaka Man|ja|日高胖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Man|1=Yutaka Hidaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510856}}, {{ill|Hasebe Eikichi|ja|長谷部鋭吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe Eikichi|1=Eikichi Hasebe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654067}}, {{ill|Takekoshi Kenzo|ja|竹腰健造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekoshi Kenzo|1=Kenzō Takekoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599406}}) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Structural Engineer = | Builder = [[:en:Obayashi Corporation|Obayashi Corporation]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Owner = Sumitomo Partnership | Structure Type = {{ill|Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|ja|鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|1=steel reinforced concrete|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649809}} | Site Area = | Building Area = Approx. 6,668 [[square meters|m{{sup|2}}]] | Total Floor Area = Approx. 35,983 m{{sup|2}} | Floors = 1 basement floor, 6 above-ground floors {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Height = Approx. 24.2 meters | Construction Start = | Completion tier 3]] (Phase 1), [[1930]] (Phase 2) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Renovation = [[1964]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}}, [[2015]]"North Hamamatsu Sumitomo Building to be preserved; SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|1=Sumitomo zaibatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885869}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|1=Sumitomo-mura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381763}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|1=Sumitomo Building|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96971400}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|1=Osaka Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11440050}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|1=Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108912361}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1926]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Kitahama, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings Occupied by Allied Forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement Architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Semba]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{wikidata link|Q11381732}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1926]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Kitahama, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings Occupied by Allied Forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement Architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Semba]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{wikidata link|Q11381732}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Sumitomo Building (Osaka)]][[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]][[zh:住友大廈]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381732]] Wikidata name: Sumitomo Building [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox building | Name = Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building
(Former: Sumitomo Building) | Former Name = Sumitomo Bank Main Building {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | image = [[File:SMBC Osaka head office.jpg|300px]] | Use = Bank, Auditorium {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}} | Former Use = Bank, Office | Architect = {{ill|Sumitomo Partnership|ja|住友合資会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Partnership|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住友財閥|1=Sumitomo zaibatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885869}} Construction Department ({{ill|Hidaka Man|ja|日高胖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidaka Man|1=Yutaka Hidaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510856}}, {{ill|Hasebe Eikichi|ja|長谷部鋭吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe Eikichi|1=Eikichi Hasebe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654067}}, {{ill|Takekoshi Kenzo|ja|竹腰健造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekoshi Kenzo|1=Kenzō Takekoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599406}}) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Structural Engineer = | Builder = [[:en:Obayashi Corporation|Obayashi Corporation]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Owner = Sumitomo Partnership | Structure Type = {{ill|Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|ja|鉄骨鉄筋コンクリート構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Steel-framed reinforced concrete structure|1=steel reinforced concrete|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11649809}} | Site Area = | Building Area = Approx. 6,668 [[square meters|m{{sup|2}}]] | Total Floor Area = Approx. 35,983 m{{sup|2}} | Floors = 1 basement floor, 6 above-ground floors {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Height = Approx. 24.2 meters | Construction Start = | Completion tier 3]] (Phase 1), [[1930]] (Phase 2) {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=130}} | Renovation = [[1964]] {{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=263}}, [[2015]]"North Hamamatsu Sumitomo Building to be preserved; SMBC Osaka Main Building emphasizes historical value" *Yomiuri Shimbun* Osaka Evening Edition, May 25, 2013 | Location Postal Code = 541-0041 | Location = [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]] [[Kita-hama]], 4-6-5 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|31|N|135|29|53|E|display=inline}} | Map Type = Osaka city | Cultural Designation = | Designation Date = }} '''Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building''' (三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル), formerly known as the '''Sumitomo Building''' (住友ビルディング) and '''Sumitomo Bank Main Building''' (住友銀行本店ビル), is located in {{ill|Kita-hama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-hama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Osaka|Chūō-ku, Osaka]]. == Overview == The building was constructed as a central hub for the {{ill|Sumitomo zaibatsu|ja|住友財閥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo zaibatsu|1=Sumitomo zaibatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10885869}}, which is now the [[:en:Sumitomo Group|Sumitomo Group]]. The area surrounding the building is home to numerous headquarters of Sumitomo Group companies, earning it the nickname "'''{{ill|Sumitomo Village|ja|住友村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Village|1=Sumitomo-mura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381763}}'''." During the pre-war period, the former Sumitomo headquarters was also located here, making this building a key structure within Sumitomo Village.{{Cite web|title=Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building |publisher=Sumitomo Group Public Relations Committee |url=https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ |access-date=2022-08-12}} The Sumitomo family business traces its origins to Kyoto during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), later relocating to Osaka in 1623. Their business flourished in metalworking, particularly in copper processing.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} Despite experiencing challenges during the turbulent transition to the Meiji government, the family modernized operations at the [[:en:Besshi copper mine|Besshi Copper Mine]] and reestablished themselves as a leading enterprise.{{Sfn|Architecture and Society|2010|p=39}} In 1885, the Sumitomo family formally established a bank, and in 1920, plans were finalized to construct a new headquarters building on their land in Kita-hama.{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=129}} The building, completed in two phases (1926 and 1930), became known for its earthquake-resistant design, a direct response to the devastating {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}. == Post-War Period == After World War II, the building was partially requisitioned by the [[:en:Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|Allied occupation of Japan]] (GHQ).{{Sfn|Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|1998|p=222}} == Recent Renovations == The building underwent significant renovations in 2015 to improve earthquake resistance while preserving its historical design. The interior was updated to include new public spaces, emphasizing the building's cultural and architectural significance.{{Cite news|title=Restoration of Mitsui Sumitomo Bank Osaka Main Building completed|publisher=Nikkei|date=2015-05-20|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXLASHC19H3T_Z10C15A5AC8000/}} == Architecture == The building is an example of early 20th-century neoclassical revival architecture, featuring details such as Ionic columns, travertine interiors, and stained glass ceilings. == Nearby == * {{ill|Sumitomo Building|ja|住友ビルディング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo Building|1=Sumitomo Building|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96971400}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Osaka Club|ja|大阪倶楽部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Club|1=Osaka Club|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11440050}} * {{ill|Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|ja|日本銀行大阪支店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|1=Bank of Japan Osaka Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108912361}} == Transportation Access == * [[:en:Higobashi Station|Higobashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line), 3 minutes' walk * [[:en:Yodoyabashi Station|Yodoyabashi Station]] (Osaka Metro Midosuji Line/Keihan Main Line), 4 minutes' walk == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|author=Sumitomo Bank History Editorial Committee|title=Sumitomo Bank Centenary History|publisher=Sumitomo Bank|date=1998-08}} * {{Cite book|author=|title=Architecture and Society|publisher=Japan Architectural Association|date=2010-09}} == External Links == * [https://www.sumitomo.gr.jp/history/related/smbc-osaka/ Official Site - Sumitomo Group Public Relations] {{translated page|ja|三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル|version=103397942|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:1926 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:1930 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Neoclassical architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[zh:%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E5%A4%A7%E5%BB%88]] [[ja:三井住友銀行大阪本店ビル]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1926]] [[Category:Buildings in Japan completed in 1930]] [[Category:Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group]] [[Category:Sumitomo Group]] [[Category:Kitahama, Osaka]] [[Category:Buildings Occupied by Allied Forces in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Modern Movement Architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Bank buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Semba]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{Commons category|Sumitomo Building (Osaka)}} [[zh:住友大廈]] {{wikidata link|Q11381732}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
